speaker: Sally
en: Excuse me, Professor.

speaker: Sally
en: Can I talk to you for a minute?

speaker: Tutor
en: Yes, do come in.

speaker: Tutor
en: So you're Sally Layman?

speaker: Sally
en: That's it.

speaker: Tutor
en: You want to discuss with me changing courses, right?

speaker: Sally
en: Yes.

speaker: Tutor
en: All right.

speaker: Tutor
en: What course are you doing at present, Sally?

speaker: Sally
en: It's Economic History.

speaker: Tutor
en: OK.

speaker: Tutor
en: And when did you begin this course?

speaker: Sally
en: Let me think.

speaker: Sally
en: Erm ... it was last September.

speaker: Tutor
en: Right.

speaker: Tutor
en: Good.

speaker: Tutor
en: Before we talk through all your problems with the course, I want to know what courses you'd like to change to.

speaker: Sally
en: Well, there're several courses that I've thought about, but my first choice would be Politics.

speaker: Tutor
en: Oh, it's one of the most popular courses in our college.

speaker: Sally
en: Yes, I know.

speaker: Sally
en: So it means there might not be places available?

speaker: Tutor
en: That's right.

speaker: Tutor
en: I'll still make a note of that.

speaker: Tutor
en: But it's better to give another option.

speaker: Sally
en: Well, I've thought of changing the course entirely to Business Studies, but then I realize it is new to me, so I think Philosophy might be a better choice.

speaker: Tutor
en: OK, that's useful to know.

speaker: Tutor
en: Then let's talk through any problems you've been having at the moment and see if there is any possibility for us to offer help.

speaker: Tutor
en: After all, with all the efforts that you've made already, it'll be a pity to give up the present course and completely start a new one.

speaker: Sally
en: Yes, I agree with that.

speaker: Sally
en: I've learnt something valuable about Economic History.

speaker: Tutor
en: Good.

speaker: Tutor
en: Well, let's talk about your problems.

speaker: Tutor
en: What do you think is your significant difficulty in the course?

speaker: Sally
en: Well, I have to say the biggest problem is that the lectures are difficult.

speaker: Tutor
en: OK.

speaker: Tutor
en: And have you tried something to overcome this problem?

speaker: Sally
en: Yes, I have.

speaker: Sally
en: I tried recording the lectures because I couldn't follow what the lecturer said.

speaker: Tutor
en: Yes, that's a good idea.

speaker: Tutor
en: And did you find it helped?

speaker: Sally
en: To a certain extent, but ...

speaker: Tutor
en: Well, recording is a good strategy, but I also advise you to take part in the discussion group with other fellow students to discuss the lectures and exchange ideas with them.

speaker: Sally
en: Yes, I've seen some of my classmates doing that.

speaker: Sally
en: I'll ask them if I can join.

speaker: Tutor
en: Good, I'm sure you'll find it helps.

speaker: Tutor
en: And any other problems?

speaker: Sally
en: Yes, the second problem is, I think there aren't enough tutorials for this course.

speaker: Tutor
en: OK.

speaker: Tutor
en: I think you can talk to your tutor about this.

speaker: Sally
en: Yes, I did that.

speaker: Sally
en: But the tutor is so busy that I must wait until she is available.

speaker: Tutor
en: Why don't you try arranging meetings ahead of time?

speaker: Sally
en: Well, I can try this way.

speaker: Tutor
en: Good.

speaker: Sally
en: But I suppose the thing that made me upset recently is my poor assignment grade.

speaker: Tutor
en: Oh, the grade is an important issue.

speaker: Sally
en: Well, I think I've tried my best to achieve a good score.

speaker: Sally
en: I did more background reading than before for the last assignment we just submitted.

speaker: Tutor
en: Good, and did you feel that helped?

speaker: Sally
en: Oh yes, I think so.

speaker: Tutor
en: Well, I think you can try another strategy, which is to visit student services, which offer a lot of helpful advice on assignment preparation.

speaker: Sally
en: Oh, I've never heard about that.

speaker: Tutor
en: I'll give you a booklet with details on this.

speaker: Tutor
en: You'll find it well worth trying.

speaker: Sally
en: OK, I'll certainly try it.

speaker: Tutor
en: OK, that's all the problem.

speaker: Tutor
en: Well, I've made a record of our discussions and suggested strategies.

speaker: Tutor
en: Typically, we advise you to try out these suggestions for a short period and then come back to discuss the situation to see whether it is improved.

speaker: Tutor
en: If not, we will start considering alternative courses.

speaker: Sally
en: OK, when shall I come back?

speaker: Tutor
en: How about in a-month time?

speaker: Sally
en: OK.

speaker: Tutor
en: That will be on the 17th of February.

speaker: Tutor
en: How about half past three in the afternoon?

speaker: Tutor
en: Is that all right for you?

speaker: Sally
en: Yes, sure.

speaker: Sally
en: And where shall I meet you?

speaker: Tutor
en: I'm afraid I will be on leave at that time.

speaker: Tutor
en: But I'll leave this record to our senior adviser, and you will meet with him then in this office.

speaker: Sally
en: OK.

speaker: Tutor
en: Good, that's it!

speaker: Tutor
en: Let's see whether things can improve, Sally.

speaker: Sally
en: OK.

speaker: Sally
en: Thanks very much!

speaker: Tutor
en: My pleasure.

speaker: Tutor
en: Bye!
